mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing equity valuations. A 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through mechanical repricing of equity cash flows.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign in more than 40% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives corporate revenue expansion and equity valuations upward. A 1-month increase in PAYEMS exceeding 150,000 persons triggers positive 5-day forward returns through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS increases exceed 150,000, or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer spending strength and forward economic momentum. When RSAFS declines month-over-month, equity investors reduce growth expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns as valuations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances following RSAFS declines exceeding 1%, or Rank IC magnitude falls below 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. Sustained INDPRO growth above trend signals robust supply-side expansion and profit margin resilience, driving positive 60-day forward returns through improved earnings quality.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the relationship exhibits regime-dependent sign reversal in more than 30% of quarterly periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points in a 3-month window, equity investors price in earnings headwinds, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances following UNRATE spikes exceeding 0.3pp, or Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both series decline simultaneously, the interaction signals a demand shock that compresses equity valuations over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both proxies to deteriorate; single-series weakness is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 5% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline > 2%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with leading indicators rolling over
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-declining instances, or the interaction effect (measured as joint signal Rank IC) is not significantly larger than the sum of individual Rank ICs (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL rises above 3% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 3%, real rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations. The mechanism requires both conditions; either alone is insufficient.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction Rank IC is not significantly larger than individual component ICs (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) represents the broadest measure of economic expansion. In expansion regimes (GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized), equity valuations expand; in contraction regimes (GDPC1 growth < 0%), valuations compress. The mechanism is regime-conditional: the same GDPC1 signal produces opposite equity returns depending on the cycle phase.
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: The sign of the relationship between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns does not flip between expansion (GDPC1 > 2% annualized) and contraction regimes, or Rank IC magnitude falls below 0.05 within either regime over any 2-year rolling window.
